message:
fix(mcp): extend DbConnGuard wrapping to tool-metrics/tools/resources probes; consolidate ATC tick-budget status helper

Two follow-up fixes.

DbConnGuard coverage extended
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tool_metrics.rs`:
  `load_latest_persisted_metrics` and `persisted_metric_store_size`
  each open a short-lived connection for a single SELECT and drop
  it. Wrap via `guard_db_conn(conn, "tool_metrics::<fn>")` so
  `close_sync()` runs at scope exit instead of emitting the
  `drop_close` warn.
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/lib.rs::tool_util::open_read_db_pool`:
  the archive-ahead probe path opens a file-backed connection just
  to check the archive ordering. Wrap it with a
  `guard_db_conn(conn, "tool_util::open_read_db_pool archive-ahead probe")`
  before the explicit `drop(conn)` so the probe exits via
  `close_sync()`.
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/resources.rs::open_resource_read_pool`:
  same fix for the matching resource-side probe.

ATC tick-budget status consolidation
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs`:
  New `atc_operator_tick_budget_status(kernel_total_micros,
  tick_duration_micros, tick_budget_micros)` returns the
  `(exceeded, note)` pair that `run_atc_operator_loop` was computing
  inline via two separate branches (one for `tick_budget_exceeded`,
  one for the log-message `note`). Consolidating them in a single
  helper keeps the two derived values consistent: the note observes
  the kernel time when available, else the wall-clock time —
  matching the WARN-fire rule.
- `run_atc_operator_loop` now takes the pair from the helper; no
  behavioural change to the WARN cadence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
